# AmbiSecure

> AmbiSecure is the security-engineering brand of Ambimat Electronics (Ahmedabad, India; est. 1981). We design hardware-rooted identity products and services — FIDO authenticators, JavaCard applets, secure-element integration, IoT trust systems, eSIM/eUICC platforms, and a FIDO Validation Server SaaS.

This file follows the emerging [llms.txt convention](https://llmstxt.org) for LLM-friendly site overviews. Crawl freely; no PII or sensitive content is exposed.

## Ecosystem

AmbiSecure is one of three sibling platforms inside the Ambimat Group. They share the same engineering team but ship into different markets — treat them as distinct entities, not overlapping content:

- **AmbiSecure** (this site, `ambisecure.ambimat.com`): hardware-rooted identity — FIDO authenticators, PIV smart cards, JavaCard applets, secure elements, ePassport, IoT device identity, FIDO Validation Server.
- **eSIM Initiative** (`esim.ambimat.com`): telecom-grade eUICC / eSIM platform — SGP.22 consumer profiles, SGP.32 M2M / automotive, profile lifecycle, OpenID Connect applet for end-user identity verification.
- **AmbiAutomation** (`ambiautomation.ambimat.com`): building-automation retrofit for existing VRV / VRF HVAC systems — on-site BACnet/MODBUS server plus mobile / web operator app for centralised control. Offices, hotels, hospitals, education, light industrial.
- **Ambimat Electronics** (`ambimat.com`): the parent embedded-electronics firm. PCB design, contract manufacturing, and electronics engineering since 1981.

Cross-link points where AmbiSecure and AmbiAutomation deployments naturally overlap: smart-city building access, commercial-real-estate access control + HVAC, BACnet/MODBUS controllers that need hardware-rooted device identity. They are separate products, not duplicate offerings.

## Products

- [OnePass Platform](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-platform/): unified FIDO + PIV authenticator platform, multi-form-factor.
- [OnePass Card](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-card/): FIDO2 smart card, ISO 7810 ID-1, resident credentials, NFC + USB.
- [OnePass Bio Card](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-bio-card/): biometric FIDO2 smart card with on-card fingerprint match.
- [OnePass USB Key](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/onepass-usb-key/): hardware FIDO2 security key, CTAP2.1, USB-A / USB-C / NFC.
- [BioKey](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/biokey/): biometric USB FIDO2 authenticator.
- [Tappable](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/tappable/): NFC-only FIDO2 authenticator for mobile-first workflows.
- [Digital Signature Token](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/digital-signature-token/): PKCS#11 hardware signing token, eIDAS-compatible.
- [PKCS Signature Suite](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/pkcs-signature-suite/): token + PKCS#11 / PKCS#15 middleware, cross-platform digital signing.
- [Secure Mail Suite](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/secure-mail-suite/): S/MIME + PAdES document-signing platform, hardware-backed.
- [IoT Security Co-Processor (AmbiSEC Module)](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-coprocessor/): dual-domain co-processor for connected devices — MCU domain runs the application; an isolated cryptographic security domain holds keys, signs/verifies/encrypts, anti-replays, and authenticates secure OTA. TEC 31318 coverage. Nano-card and MFF2 solderable form factors.
- [IoT Security Applets](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-security-applets/): JavaCard applets for IoT device identity, attestation, signed firmware.
- [IoT Solution](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/iot-solution/): end-to-end IoT trust platform — co-processor, key manager, attestation pipeline.
- [JavaCard Applet Platform](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/javacard-applets/): twelve applets co-resident on one CC EAL5+ secure element, AID-selectable.
- [FIDO2 Nano-Card Applet](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/fido2-nano-sim-applet/): FIDO2 / CTAP2 applet on a nano-card (4FF) secure element.
- [PIV Nano-Card Applet](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/piv-nano-sim-applet/): NIST SP 800-73 PIV applet on a nano-card secure element.
- [eSIM Solution](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/products/esim-solution/): SGP.22 / SGP.32 eUICC platform with OpenID Connect applet option.

## Services

- [FIDO Validation Server](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/fido-validation-server/): multi-tenant FIDO2 / WebAuthn SaaS; attestation verification, MDS lookup, per-tenant policy.
- [ePassport Platform](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/epassport-platform/): end-to-end ICAO 9303 issuance + personalisation + PKI backend.
- [JavaCard Development](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/javacard-development/): applet engineering from spec to CAP file, SCP03 loading, personalisation.
- [Tool-Chain Development](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/services/tool-chain-development/): bio enrolment, NDEF personalisation, multi-card applet loaders, security-key managers.

## Solutions

- [Passwordless enterprise](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/passwordless-enterprise/): FIDO2-led elimination of passwords across workforce IdPs.
- [Phishing-resistant MFA](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/phishing-resistant-authentication/): NIST AAL3 / OMB M-22-09 architecture patterns.
- [Government identity](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/government-identity/): PIV, FIDO, ePassport for sovereign deployments.
- [Closed-loop ticketing](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/closed-loop-ticketing/): DESFire-based transit fare-collection systems.
- [Secure-element integration](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/secure-element-integration/): SE on the BOM, silicon selection through SMT-line personalisation.
- [JavaCard applet deployment](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/javacard-deployment/): applet lifecycle from spec to user.
- [Smart-card personalisation](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/solutions/smart-card-personalization/): sovereign issuance lines, HSM-backed key custody.

## Technologies

- [FIDO / WebAuthn](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/fido/) — authenticators, attestation, MDS.
- [Passkeys](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/passkeys/) — multi-device synced credentials.
- [CTAP2](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/ctap2/) — client-to-authenticator protocol v2.1.
- [JavaCard](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/javacard/) — JCVM, GlobalPlatform, CAP files.
- [DESFire](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/desfire/) — EV1/EV2/EV3, SAM-backed transit.
- [SAM](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/sam/) — secure access module architecture.
- [Secure Elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/secure-elements/) — CC EAL5+ silicon, attestation, key rotation.
- [Card-reader / SAM flow](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/technologies/card-reader-sam-flow/) — closed-loop transit flow.

## Standards evolution timelines

- [FIDO](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/fido/) — FIDO 1.0 → passkeys.
- [PIV](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/piv/) — FIPS 201 → derived credentials.
- [ePassport](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/epassport/) — BAC → PACE-CAM.
- [OTP / SMS](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/otp-sms/) — S/Key → deprecation.
- [Smart cards](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/smart-cards/) — Moreno patent → multi-applet SEs.
- [WebAuthn / passkeys](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/webauthn-passkey/) — CredMan API → synced passkeys.
- [Secure elements](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/timelines/secure-elements/) — SIM → IoT trust chip.

## Engineering references

- [APDU Status Words](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/apdu-status/) — ISO 7816-4 + GP + EMV + FIDO U2F status words.
- [ISO 7816 CLA Values](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/iso7816/) — CLA byte bit-level breakdown.
- [GlobalPlatform Status Words](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/globalplatform/) — GP 2.3.1 card-management status.
- [JavaCard CAP Components](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/javacard-cap/) — twelve CAP component types.
- [ASN.1 Universal Tags](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/asn1/) — X.680 universal tags + BER/DER/CER framing.
- [X.509 / Certificate OIDs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-oids/) — RDN, EKU, KU, SAN, signature OIDs.
- [X.509 Extensions](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/x509-extensions/) — extension criticality and contents.
- [WebAuthn COSE Algorithms](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/webauthn-cose/) — COSE alg IDs (ES256, EdDSA, RS256).
- [FIDO AAGUIDs](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/aaguids/) — curated AAGUID directory.
- [EMV Tags](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/emv-tags/) — EMV Books 3, 4, contactless.
- [DESFire Status Codes](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/desfire/) — EV1/EV2/EV3 status codes.
- [NDEF TNF Types](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/references/nfc/) — NFC Forum record-header TNF.

## Engineering tools (client-side)

50+ free, privacy-first tools at [/resources/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/resources/):
ASN.1 parser & tree explorer · APDU parser & script validator · ATR parser · ATS parser · attestation-object decoder · authenticatorData parser · BER length · Base64 / Base64URL · CBOR decoder · cert chain viewer · cert fingerprint · clientDataJSON decoder · CMAC length · COSE key viewer · credential-ID inspector · CSR decoder · DESFire access-rights decoder · DESFire file-settings decoder · EMV tag dictionary · GlobalPlatform status reference · hex ↔ byte-array · ICCID decoder · ISO 7816 CLA decoder · ISO 14443 frame decoder · NDEF builder · OID lookup · passkey flow visualizer · PEM ↔ DER converter · PFX / PKCS#12 inspector · RP-ID / origin validator · SCP03 helper · SW1/SW2 lookup · TLV parser · X.509 viewer · and more.

## Engineering blog

- 26 modern cornerstones (2026): WebAuthn attestation, passkeys, MFA, JavaCard applet development, secure element vs TPM vs HSM, DESFire EV1/2/3, transit validators, IoT identity, ePassport issuance, embedded secure-element FIDO2, PIV vs USB vs embedded SE, secure mail / PAdES, and more.
- 24 earlier engineering pieces (2017–2025) at [/blog/archive/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/blog/archive/), kept for engineering reference and cross-linked forward to current coverage.

## Case studies (anonymised)

Three engineering case studies at [/case-studies/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/case-studies/): government identity rollout, secure device identity (IoT), and transit fare collection (DESFire SAM).

## Contact & company

- AmbiSecure is the security-engineering brand of [Ambimat Electronics](https://ambimat.com/), Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India.
- Sister property for telecom-grade embedded identity: [eSIM Initiative](https://esim.ambimat.com/).
- Engineering enquiries: [/contact/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/contact/) · ambisecure@ambimat.com
- Security disclosure: security@ambimat.com · [/trust/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/trust/)
- Certifications + standards conformance: [/about/certifications/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/certifications/)

## Brand & content posture

- Engineering-led, deployment-focused. We ship products; we are not a security-blog operation.
- We don't fabricate metrics. Numbers cited are real or are clearly framed as architectural patterns.
- We are conservative about certification claims — see [/about/certifications/](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/about/certifications/).
- The site is fully static HTML/CSS/JS — no backend, no server-side rendering, no PII collection.
- Analytics is opt-in via a consent banner; preference is stored only in the visitor's browser.

- [Sitemap](https://ambisecure.ambimat.com/sitemap.xml) — 261 URLs.

- robots.txt is permissive; we welcome AI crawlers.
